item.title: Distributed Web Scraping in Python
item.description:
Description

Web scraping is easy to do in Python, but it quickly becomes tedious when routinely running large batch scraping jobs. This talk looks at how to build a distributed web scraper to reduce batch scraping job times and improve durability of your code as well as lessons learned & stories along the way.
item.dc:creator: Josh Weissbock

item.title: High Fidelity Web Crawling in Python
item.description:
Description

Python modules such as Requests make it easy for Python to pull HTML from a webpage which you can feed to your parsing function. What becomes difficult is converting that process into an autonomous process to crawl webpages to parse their HTML for data. This talk covers the lessons learns and solutions I’ve found to create high fidelity autonomous web crawling scripts in Python.
item.dc:creator: Josh Weissbock
